blockers.title: Waitlisted members can be checked in and receive attendance/standing credit
blockers.evidence: `convex/admin/events.ts` accepts any non-cancelled registration in `checkIn` and only rejects missing, cancelled, or wrong-event records before patching the state to `attended` or `no_show`. When outcome is `attended`, it writes an `event_checked_in` activity and calls standing promotion logic. The admin UI exposes Mark attended/No show for every non-cancelled row in `src/admin/views/EventRegistrationsView.tsx`, including `waitlisted` rows. This lets a member who never received a seat bypass the capacity/waitlist promotion path and still get attendance evidence and possible Active standing credit.
blockers.required_fix: Restrict event check-in to actual seat-holding registrations, with corrections allowed only for existing `registered`, `attended`, or `no_show` states. If waitlisted attendance must be supported, first promote the member through the same capacity, lane, lifecycle, and audit checks used by waitlist promotion. Hide check-in actions for waitlisted rows and add regression tests proving waitlisted rows cannot become attended/no_show or receive standing activity directly.

non_blocking_notes: `git diff --check main...HEAD` passed, aside from sandbox xcrun cache warnings.
`npm test` and `npm run check` could not be fully verified in this read-only sandbox because they attempted to write generated files under `node_modules/.vite-temp` and `.astro/content.d.ts`.
No real secrets were found in the diff, and I did not find diff-added member-facing em dashes.

spec_compliance.criterion: Events preserve capacity, waitlist, check-in, and standing invariants end-to-end.
spec_compliance.evidence: The check-in path can mark `waitlisted` registrations as attended/no_show and can write attendance activity, bypassing the registered-seat requirement.

spec_compliance.criterion: Under-18 and restricted_unknown members do not see or RSVP to restricted adult events.
spec_compliance.evidence: Server-side event listing, detail, RSVP, and pass queries apply member-lane checks; minors only see youth events and restricted_unknown sees no events.

spec_compliance.criterion: Opportunities enforce lane, audience, profile completeness, deadline, and own-history visibility rules.
spec_compliance.evidence: Board/apply queries gate active standard/ally members, profile completion, open audience, and deadlines, while own application history remains separately visible.

spec_compliance.criterion: Directory exposes only approved directory fields and excludes minors/restricted_unknown.
spec_compliance.evidence: Directory queries require an active adult lane and return public profile fields without email, mobile, DOB, gender, or bio.

spec_compliance.criterion: Admin and super-admin boundaries are enforced for sensitive panels.
spec_compliance.evidence: Admin routes use explicit admin checks, while certificate management and platform health use super-admin checks.
Admin contact reveal is separated from member detail and writes an audit entry before exposing contact data.
Report queries return counts and grouped totals, not member-level rows.

spec_compliance.criterion: Brand locks for added member-facing strings are honoured.
spec_compliance.evidence: No diff-added member-facing em dashes were found in the reviewed portal/admin strings.
Typecheck passed, but `npm test` and `npm run check` could not complete in the read-only sandbox because required generated files could not be written.

summary: Do not ship. The slice mostly implements the expected authorization, privacy, and lane gates, but the event check-in path breaks the seat/waitlist/standing contract by allowing waitlisted members to be marked as attended directly.
